Container.Name: peaks
Container.Overview: Opus/peaks — learns your taste from example stills, finds the matching moments across a Stash library, writes them back as scene markers, and plays them on a continuously-cycling "megaboard" grid (port 8800). All other commands (embed, score, label, train) run from this container's console. Requires the Nvidia Driver plugin + --runtime=nvidia for GPU embedding. IMPORTANT: the Media path below must be mounted at the SAME container path your Stash container uses, so file paths reported by Stash resolve here. The embedding model (DINOv2 / CLIP) and sampling knobs (mode, interval, GPU decode, workers, timeout) are set per-run from the Dashboard's Embed → Advanced panel — no restart needed — so they are NOT container variables here.

Config.Name: Labeler
Config.Description: The Tier-2 frame-rating page (only live while 'peaks label' runs).

Config.Name: Config / data
Config.Description: Settings, embedding cache, models, labels, reference stills.

Config.Name: Media (match Stash's container path!)
Config.Description: Your video library. CRITICAL: set the host path to the same share Stash uses, and edit the container path ('/data') to whatever path your STASH container mounts it at — check your Stash container's settings. Read-only is fine.

Config.Name: STASH_URL
Config.Description: Base URL of your Stash server.

Config.Name: STASH_API_KEY
Config.Description: Stash > Settings > Security > API Key. Leave empty if Stash has no auth.

Config.Name: PEAKS_LIBRARY_PATH
Config.Description: Only look at files whose path starts with this (blank = whole library). Use it to exclude VR etc. — e.g. /data/Rando. Must be the CONTAINER path (matches Stash's), including subfolders.

Config.Name: PEAKS_DEVICE
Config.Description: Compute device for embedding. Leave BLANK for auto (GPU when present, CPU when not) — recommended, so it survives the GPU being borrowed/returned without editing. Force with 'cuda' or 'cpu'.

Config.Name: PEAKS_SCENE_TIMEOUT
Config.Description: Per-scene sampling ceiling in seconds — a corrupt/slow-seek file is killed after this so it can't stall a run. 300 clears most slow files; raise to 600 if some good scenes still get killed. Applies to scheduled + `peaks fix` runs (the GUI Embed panel can override it per-run). 0 disables.

Config.Name: PEAKS_EMBED_EVERY_HOURS
Config.Description: If > 0, the web app auto-embeds newly-added scenes on this schedule (hours). Great for picking up new scenes on CPU after the GPU goes back to the VM. 0 = manual only.

Config.Name: PEAKS_SYNC
Config.Description: After each recurring embed pass, reconcile the cache with Stash: scenes that MOVED between subfolders get their stored path refreshed (no re-embedding — the cache is keyed by a stable file fingerprint). true/false.

Config.Name: PEAKS_PRUNE
Config.Description: Also delete cache entries for scenes REMOVED from Stash during the recurring pass. Off by default (destructive — a bad Stash response can't wipe your cache). The manual 'peaks sync' and the GUI 'Sync now' button prune regardless. true/false.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
Config.Description: Which GPU(s) the container may use ('all', or a GPU UUID from Settings > Nvidia Driver).

Config.Name: NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES
Config.Description: Leave as 'all' (compute for embedding + video for NVDEC decode).
